title: HLA-A whole-allele dG37 anti-correlates with mRNA expression across protein-distinct alleles

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'HLA-A whole-allele dG37 anti-correlates with mRNA expression across protein-dist'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: IPD-IMGT/HLA v3.56.0 genomic sequences. Endpoint lookup_hla_alleles. HLA-A subset only.
Bettens et al. 2022 per-allele HLA RNA-seq TPM in peripheral blood from 63 donors. Restricted here to HLA-A protein-distinct alleles with dG37 measurable.

premises.note: Premise restricted to the 19 HLA-A alleles where Bettens TPM and IMGT genomic sequence both exist and pass QC.

source.note: dG37 (kcal/mol) computed via compute_region_biophysics over each allele's full genomic sequence (UTR + introns + exons). Whole-allele scope per Phase 1 design; later phases test per-region restriction.

statistics.name: spearman_rho
statistics.note: Pre-specified H1 test (Bonferroni-corrected family); negative direction was predicted by Phase 1 hypothesis.

statistics.name: min_abs_rho_across_leave_one_out
statistics.note: Smallest |rho| observed across all 19 leave-one-out subsamples. All subsamples retained the negative sign.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts license the conclusion: (1) effect-size threshold |rho| > 0.6 separates this from the 0.3–0.4 noise band typical for n=19 in HLA TPM data; (2) p < 0.001 survives the Phase 1 Bonferroni correction across 6 pre-specified tests; (3) leave-one-out jackknife |rho| > 0.5 minimum confirms the signal is not driven by a single outlier allele (e.g., A*33:03, audited separately as Supp Fig S1).
inference.failure_mode: If |rho| dropped below 0.5 after dropping any single allele, the n=19 power floor would dominate and the claim would be revoked. Current min jackknife |rho| = 0.61 is the load-bearing margin.

conclusion.assertion: Across protein-distinct HLA-A alleles (Bettens 2022 cohort), whole-allele mean stacking dG37 anti-correlates with peripheral-blood mRNA expression at Spearman rho = -0.711 (p = 6.5e-4, n = 19), robust to single-allele exclusion (jackknife |rho| >= 0.61).

scope.scale_note: Whole-allele scope. The within-region decomposition (5'UTR / exon / intron / 3'UTR) is encoded as separate claims; this claim is the bulk-allele headline correlation.

confidence.note: p = 6.5e-4; per-allele leverage diagnostics in Supp Fig S1 (A*33:03 audit).

external_assumptions.statement: Bettens et al. 2022 per-allele TPM is a faithful proxy for per-allele transcript abundance in peripheral blood.
Whole-allele dG37 mean is a meaningful summary of stacking thermodynamics despite mixing UTR / intron / exon contributions.
n=19 is sufficient for Spearman rho point estimation when the effect is large; CI is wide but the sign is stable under jackknife.
